methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)

data.narrative:
Dawn Aerospace, a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $30M+, was valued using two applicable triangulation methods against a relevant peer universe. The comparable set includes four private-sector peers at the same stage—Aalyria ($1.3B), Stoke Space ($1.0B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—alongside five public Space & Aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) that establish sector multiples, though direct public-comp anchoring could not be applied due to unavailable revenue data.

The Stage-Progression method, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $30M raised proxy, implies a valuation of $57M. The Peer-Median approach across the four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $700M, reflecting the median of a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. These two methods produce a wide dispersion, driven largely by the high concentration and variance of private aerospace valuations in the current market.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $48M to $805M, with a median of $378M. The material gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects structural uncertainty: whether Dawn Aerospace aligns with lower-funded cohorts or commands aerospace-sector multiples justified by technology differentiation or market position. Key sensitivities include the applicability of peer multiples given likely differences in development stage, path to revenue, and capital efficiency relative to the comparison set.
